docs.text: Plane ¶ A simple and dynamic asynchronous Python wrapper for the Ravy API. Features ¶ Easy usability 100% API coverage Unit tested (soon™️) Pyright type compatible Installation ¶ Compatible with Python 3.7+ Not yet available on PyPI python3 -m pip install git+https://github.com/GoogleGenius/plane.git Usage ¶ # Import required packages import asyncio import plane async def main () -> None : # Construct a plane client object client = plane . Client ( "token" ) # Replace "token" with your API key # Make a simple request to get token information token_info = await client . tokens . get_token () print ( token_info . token_type ) # Print the token type: "ravy" | "ksoft" # Close and teardown the client await client . close () # Start the event loop and run the main function asyncio . run ( main ()) Contributing ¶ Feel free to create pull requests and issues. Just be civil, kind, and respectful. This is my first library, so if you have any suggestions or questions, please let me know! Reach out via GitHub Issues or Discord GoogleGenius#7777 .
docs.title: Plane

docs.text: # Import required packages import asyncio import plane async def main () -> None : # Construct a plane client object client = plane . Client ( "token" ) # Replace "token" with your API key # Make a simple request to get token information token_info = await client . tokens . get_token () print ( token_info . token_type ) # Print the token type: "ravy" | "ksoft" # Close and teardown the client await client . close () # Start the event loop and run the main function asyncio . run ( main ())
docs.title: Usage

docs.text: Feel free to create pull requests and issues. Just be civil, kind, and respectful. This is my first library, so if you have any suggestions or questions, please let me know! Reach out via GitHub Issues or Discord GoogleGenius#7777 .
docs.title: Contributing

docs.text: Getting Started ¶ Quick Example ¶ # Import required packages import asyncio import plane async def main () -> None : # Construct a plane client object client = plane . Client ( "token" ) # Replace "token" with your API key # Make a simple request to get token information token_info = await client . tokens . get_token () print ( token_info . token_type ) # Print the token type: "ravy" | "ksoft" # Close and teardown the client await client . close () # Start the event loop and run the main function asyncio . run ( main ()) KSoft Tokens ¶ If you have a KSoft token, you can simply pass it to the constructor for the plane.client.Client . Please note that KSoft tokens are not compatible with any endpoints other than ksoft and tokens . Phisherman Token ¶ You might have noticed there is no available kwarg to set the phisherman.gg token using the urls endpoint. This is because you can instead set it directly to the plane.client.Client using the plane.client.Client.set_phisherman_token() method. This also returns the plane.client.Client object so you can chain calls. # Assume boilerplate is already set up client = plane . Client ( "token" ) . set_phisherman_token ( "phisherman_token" ) website_info = await client . urls . get_website ( "https://example.com" ) Permissions ¶ The API wrapper automatically validates your token's permissions upon the first method call. If you attempt to use an API method that you do not have permission to use, the library will raise a plane.api.errors.AccessException . This information is currently stored internally, but not publicly accessible. To manually check for permissions, call the plane.client.Client.tokens.get_token() method and use the access property. # Assume boilerplate is already set up token_info = await client . tokens . get_token () permissions = token_info . access Error Handling ¶ You can catch the defined errors in the plane.api.errors module and handle them appropriately. # Assume boilerplate is already set up try : token_info = await client . tokens . get_token () except plane . HTTPException as e : # Generic HTTP error if e . status == 429 : print ( f "Encountered { e . status } : we are being ratelimited by Cloudflare!" ) else : print ( f "Encountered HTTP error: ( { e . status } ) - { e . exc_data } !" ) try : website_info = await client . urls . get_website ( "https://example.com" ) except plane . AccessException as e : # Access denied print ( f "This errored as the endpoint route needed { e . required } permission!" ) More Information ¶ Check the API reference for more information as well as the official Ravy API documentation: Reference Ravy Docs
docs.title: Getting Started

docs.text: # Import required packages import asyncio import plane async def main () -> None : # Construct a plane client object client = plane . Client ( "token" ) # Replace "token" with your API key # Make a simple request to get token information token_info = await client . tokens . get_token () print ( token_info . token_type ) # Print the token type: "ravy" | "ksoft" # Close and teardown the client await client . close () # Start the event loop and run the main function asyncio . run ( main ())
docs.title: Quick Example

docs.text: If you have a KSoft token, you can simply pass it to the constructor for the plane.client.Client . Please note that KSoft tokens are not compatible with any endpoints other than ksoft and tokens .
docs.title: KSoft Tokens

docs.text: You might have noticed there is no available kwarg to set the phisherman.gg token using the urls endpoint. This is because you can instead set it directly to the plane.client.Client using the plane.client.Client.set_phisherman_token() method. This also returns the plane.client.Client object so you can chain calls. # Assume boilerplate is already set up client = plane . Client ( "token" ) . set_phisherman_token ( "phisherman_token" ) website_info = await client . urls . get_website ( "https://example.com" )
docs.title: Phisherman Token

docs.text: The API wrapper automatically validates your token's permissions upon the first method call. If you attempt to use an API method that you do not have permission to use, the library will raise a plane.api.errors.AccessException . This information is currently stored internally, but not publicly accessible. To manually check for permissions, call the plane.client.Client.tokens.get_token() method and use the access property. # Assume boilerplate is already set up token_info = await client . tokens . get_token () permissions = token_info . access
docs.title: Permissions

docs.text: You can catch the defined errors in the plane.api.errors module and handle them appropriately. # Assume boilerplate is already set up try : token_info = await client . tokens . get_token () except plane . HTTPException as e : # Generic HTTP error if e . status == 429 : print ( f "Encountered { e . status } : we are being ratelimited by Cloudflare!" ) else : print ( f "Encountered HTTP error: ( { e . status } ) - { e . exc_data } !" ) try : website_info = await client . urls . get_website ( "https://example.com" ) except plane . AccessException as e : # Access denied print ( f "This errored as the endpoint route needed { e . required } permission!" )
docs.title: Error Handling

docs.text: Module containing the client interfacing for interactions. Client ¶ The client interface for interacting with the Ravy API. ATTRIBUTE DESCRIPTION closed Whether or not the client is closed. TYPE: bool avatars The avatars endpoint. TYPE: Avatars guilds The guilds endpoint. TYPE: Guilds ksoft The ksoft endpoint. TYPE: KSoft users The users endpoint. TYPE: Users urls The urls endpoint. TYPE: URLs tokens The tokens endpoint. TYPE: Tokens __init__ ( token : str ) -> None ¶ PARAMETER DESCRIPTION token The token used to authenticate with the API. TYPE: str avatars () -> Avatars property ¶ The avatars endpoint. close () -> None async ¶ Closes the client, shutting down the underlying HTTP client. closed () -> bool property ¶ Whether or not the client is closed. guilds () -> Guilds property ¶ The guilds endpoint. ksoft () -> KSoft property ¶ The ksoft endpoint. set_phisherman_token ( token : str ) -> Client ¶ Sets the phisherman token for the client. PARAMETER DESCRIPTION token The phisherman token to set. TYPE: str RETURNS DESCRIPTION Client The client with the phisherman token set. tokens () -> Tokens property ¶ The tokens endpoint. urls () -> URLs property ¶ The urls endpoint. users () -> Users property ¶ The users endpoint.
docs.title: Client

docs.text: Exceptions raised when an error is encountered during API calls. AccessError ¶ Bases: Exception A class denoting an exception raised when required permission is not satisfied. ATTRIBUTE DESCRIPTION required The required permission for a path route. TYPE: str __init__ ( required : str ) -> None ¶ PARAMETER DESCRIPTION required The permission that was needed. TYPE: str required () -> str property ¶ The required permission for a path route. BadRequestError ¶ Bases: HTTPError A class denoting an exception raised when a bad request is made. ATTRIBUTE DESCRIPTION exc_data The error data returned by the Ravy API. TYPE: str | dict [ str , Any ] __init__ ( exc_data : str | dict [ str , Any ]) -> None ¶ PARAMETER DESCRIPTION exc_data The error data returned by the Ravy API. TYPE: str | dict [ str , Any ] ForbiddenError ¶ Bases: HTTPError A class denoting an exception raised when a forbidden request is made. ATTRIBUTE DESCRIPTION exc_data The error data returned by the Ravy API. TYPE: str | dict [ str , Any ] __init__ ( exc_data : str | dict [ str , Any ]) -> None ¶ PARAMETER DESCRIPTION exc_data The error data returned by the Ravy API. TYPE: str | dict [ str , Any ] HTTPError ¶ Bases: Exception A base class for all HTTP exceptions. PARAMETER DESCRIPTION status The HTTP status code of the response. TYPE: int exc_data The error data returned by the Ravy API. TYPE: str | dict [ str , Any ] ATTRIBUTE DESCRIPTION status The HTTP status code of the response. TYPE: int exc_data The error data returned by the Ravy API. TYPE: str | dict [ str , Any ] __init__ ( status : int , exc_data : str | dict [ str , Any ]) -> None ¶ PARAMETER DESCRIPTION status The HTTP status code of the response. TYPE: int exc_data The error data returned by the Ravy API. TYPE: str | dict [ str , Any ] exc_data () -> str | dict [ str , Any ] property ¶ The error data returned by the Ravy API. status () -> int property ¶ The HTTP status code of the response. NotFoundError ¶ Bases: HTTPError A class denoting an exception raised when a resource is not found. ATTRIBUTE DESCRIPTION exc_data The error data returned by the Ravy API. TYPE: str | dict [ str , Any ] __init__ ( exc_data : str | dict [ str , Any ]) -> None ¶ PARAMETER DESCRIPTION exc_data The error data returned by the Ravy API. TYPE: str | dict [ str , Any ] TooManyRequestsError ¶ Bases: HTTPError A class denoting an exception raised when a request is made too frequently. ATTRIBUTE DESCRIPTION exc_data The error data returned by the Ravy API. TYPE: str | dict [ str , Any ] __init__ ( exc_data : str | dict [ str , Any ]) -> None ¶ PARAMETER DESCRIPTION exc_data The error data returned by the Ravy API. TYPE: str | dict [ str , Any ] UnauthorizedError ¶ Bases: HTTPError A class denoting an exception raised when an unauthorized request is made. ATTRIBUTE DESCRIPTION exc_data The error data returned by the Ravy API. TYPE: str | dict [ str , Any ] __init__ ( exc_data : str | dict [ str , Any ]) -> None ¶ PARAMETER DESCRIPTION exc_data The error data returned by the Ravy API. TYPE: str | dict [ str , Any ]

docs.text: Implementations for the avatars endpoint. Avatars ¶ Bases: HTTPAwareEndpoint A class with implementations for the avatars endpoint. check_avatar ( avatar : str | bytes , threshold : float = 0.97 , method : Literal [ ssim , phash ] = 'phash' ) -> CheckAvatarResponse async ¶ Check if avatar is fraudulent. PARAMETER DESCRIPTION avatar Link to the avatar, should start with "cdn.discordapp.com" or the avatar to query, as an octet stream. TYPE: str | bytes threshold How similar the avatar needs to be for it to match (0-1, default 0.97). TYPE: float DEFAULT: 0.97 method Which method to use for matching the avatars ("ssim" or "phash", default is "phash"). TYPE: Literal [ ssim , phash ] DEFAULT: 'phash' RAISES DESCRIPTION TypeError If any parameters are of invalid types. ValueError If any parameters are invalid values. RETURNS DESCRIPTION CheckAvatarResponse A model response from plane.api.endpoints.avatars.Avatars.check_avatar . Located as plane.api.models.avatars.CheckAvatarResponse .
docs.title: Avatars

docs.text: Implementations for the urls endpoint. URLs ¶ Bases: HTTPAwareEndpoint A class with implementations for the urls endpoint. edit_website ( url : str , * , is_fraudulent : bool , message : str , encode : bool = True ) -> None async ¶ Edit website information. PARAMETER DESCRIPTION url The url-encoded url to set data for. TYPE: str is_fraudulent Whether the website is fraudulent. TYPE: bool message An informational message about the website. TYPE: str encode Whether to url-encode the parameter url . TYPE: bool DEFAULT: True RAISES DESCRIPTION TypeError If any parameters are of invalid types. ValueError If any parameters are invalid values. get_website ( url : str , * , author : int | None = None , phisherman_user : int | None = None ) -> GetWebsiteResponse async ¶ Get website information. PARAMETER DESCRIPTION url The url-encoded url to look up. TYPE: str author Optional, the user that posted the message containing this URL (for auto banning, requires admin.users). TYPE: int | None DEFAULT: None phisherman_user Optional, required if plane.client.Client.set_phisherman_token is called, Discord user ID of the token owner. TYPE: int | None DEFAULT: None RAISES DESCRIPTION TypeError If any parameters are of invalid types. ValueError If any parameters are invalid values. RETURNS DESCRIPTION GetWebsiteResponse A model response from plane.api.endpoints.urls.URLs.get_website . Located as plane.api.models.urls.GetWebsiteResponse .
docs.title: Urls

docs.text: Generic models for ban entries. BanEntryRequest ¶ A generic model for ban entry requests. PARAMETER DESCRIPTION provider Source for where the user or guild is banned. TYPE: str reason Why the user or guild is banned. TYPE: str moderator User ID of the responsible moderator, usually Discord. TYPE: int reason_key Machine-readable version of the reason - only present for providers ravy and dservices. TYPE: str | None DEFAULT: None ATTRIBUTE DESCRIPTION provider Source for where the user or guild is banned. TYPE: str reason Why the user or guild is banned. TYPE: str moderator User ID of the responsible moderator, usually Discord. TYPE: int reason_key Machine-readable version of the reason - only present for providers ravy and dservices.
